title: Smart Robots in Our Daily Lives
passage:
Smart Machines in Our Homes and Schools

Today, we have many smart machines in our lives. These machines use Artificial Intelligence, or AI. AI helps machines think, learn, and make decisions. We can see AI and robots in our homes, schools, and hospitals every day.

In many modern homes, people use small robot vacuum cleaners. These robots are round and flat. They move on the floor and clean up dust and dirt. They can find their way around chairs and tables. When they finish cleaning, they go back to their charging stations. Many people buy these robots because they save a lot of time. People can relax while the robot cleans the house.

In some schools, teachers use small robots to help children learn. These robots can talk, sing, and play spelling games with kids. They help children learn English, science, and math. Children love these robots because they are fun and friendly. However, robots cannot replace human teachers. Human teachers can understand children's feelings and help them feel happy, but robots cannot do this.

In big hospitals, doctors and nurses use special robots. Some robots help doctors during difficult operations. These robots are very precise and do not make mistakes. Other robots carry medicine, water, and food to patients in different rooms. This helps nurses because they do not have to carry heavy things, and they have more time to talk to patients.

In the future, we will see more robots around us. Some scientists are making robot pets. These robot dogs and cats can bark, purr, and play with older people. They do not need food, walks, or visits to the doctor, so they are very easy to keep. AI and robots are changing our world, and they will make our lives much easier in the future.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What does AI help machines do?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 1 states: 'AI helps machines think, learn, and make decisions.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Why do many people buy robot vacuum cleaners?

options.A: Because they save a lot of time
options.C: Because they can talk to people

questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'Many people buy these robots because they save a lot of time.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Why do children love the school robots?

options.A: Because they are big and scary
options.B: Because they are fun and friendly

questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'Children love these robots because they are fun and friendly.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What can human teachers do that robots cannot do?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'Human teachers can understand children's feelings and help them feel happy, but robots cannot do this.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE if the statement agrees with the information, FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.
questions.text: Robot vacuum cleaners go back to their charging stations when they finish cleaning.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'When they finish cleaning, they go back to their charging stations.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE if the statement agrees with the information, FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.
questions.text: School robots can teach children how to swim.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 mentions they help children learn English, science, and math, but there is no information about teaching children how to swim.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE if the statement agrees with the information, FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.
questions.text: Hospital robots carry heavy things like medicine, water, and food.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'Other robots carry medicine, water, and food... This helps nurses because they do not have to carry heavy things...'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE if the statement agrees with the information, FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.
questions.text: Robot pets need to visit the doctor once a year.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 5 states: 'They do not need food, walks, or visits to the doctor...'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Robot vacuum cleaners are round and ______.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'These robots are round and flat.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Some robots help doctors during difficult ______.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'Some robots help doctors during difficult operations.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Scientists are making robot ______ to play with older people.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 5 states: 'Some scientists are making robot pets. These robot dogs and cats can bark, purr, and play with older people.'

vocabulary.meaning: Trí tuệ nhân tạo (công nghệ giúp máy tính tự suy nghĩ và học hỏi)

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài cho trình độ A1-A2

1. Dạng bài Multiple Choice (Trắc nghiệm): Các câu hỏi ở trình độ này rất trực tiếp. Hãy tìm từ khóa trong câu hỏi (ví dụ: 'AI help', 'vacuum cleaners', 'human teachers') rồi dò tìm trong đoạn văn. Đáp án đúng sẽ chứa các từ giống hệt hoặc cực kỳ gần gũi với bài đọc.

2. Dạng bài True/False/Not Given: Đọc kỹ câu nhận định. Nếu thông tin trái ngược hoàn toàn (ví dụ: bài viết bảo 'không cần đi bác sĩ' nhưng câu hỏi bảo 'cần đi bác sĩ một lần một năm'), chọn FALSE. Nếu thông tin hoàn toàn không được nhắc tới trong bài (như việc robot dạy bơi), chọn NOT GIVEN.

3. Dạng bài điền từ vào chỗ trống: Chú ý giới hạn từ (NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS - không quá 2 từ). Tìm các từ đứng trước và sau chỗ trống trong bài đọc để xác định từ cần điền chính xác 100% từ trong văn bản, không tự ý thay đổi dạng từ.
